content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


July 25, 1990


Anthony J. Miller
Office of the Secretary of State
1230 J Street
Sacramento, CA  95814

Re:  Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-433

Dear Mr. Miller:

You have requested advice on behalf of the Secretary of State
with respect to application of the mass mailing provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

QUESTION

Is it permissible to include the words "Secretary of State"
in directions to voters for the return of a questionnaire which
will be included in the November 1990 General Election Ballot
Pamphlet?

CONCLUSION

The questionnaire may not include a reference to the
"Secretary of State" in the mailing instructions.

FACTS

In conjunction with the political science department of the
University of California, the Secretary of State will include a
questionnaire in the November 1990 General Ballot Pamphlet to
survey voters as to ways improve the pamphlet.  Voters who
complete the questionnaire must provide their own envelopes.  The
questionnaire will provide the correct return address to be used.
You have been advised that the questionnaire should be returned to
an "official" government agency, because that will increase

___________
1  Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-90-433
Page 2

participation. Therefore, you would like to instruct voters to
return the questionnaire to "Secretary of State, P.O. Box xxxxx,
Sacramento, California 958xx," provided this reference to
"Secretary of State" is permissible under the Act.

ANALYSIS

Section 89001 of the Act provides that "no newsletter or
other mass mailing shall be sent at public expense." A mass mail-
ing is defined as two hundred or more substantially similar pieces
of mail. (Section 82004.5.) Regulation 18901 clarifies that the
purpose of Section 89001 is to prevent elected officers from using
public funds to send out newsletters and other mass mailings at
public expense. A mass mailing may not be sent at public expense
if it contains the name, office, photograph or any other reference
to an elected officer affiliated with the agency producing or
sending the mailing and is prepared or sent in cooperation,
consultation, coordination or concert with the elected officer.
(Regulation 18901(a).)

The use of the term "Secretary of State" in the mailing
instructions is a reference to the elected officer. Regulation
18901 sets forth a series of limited exceptions. These exceptions
include an exemption to permit reference to an elected officer in
the letterhead or envelopes of the stationery of the agency.
(Regulation 18901(b)(1), copy enclosed.) The exemption does not
extend to permitting reference to the elected officer in mailing
instructions.

Therefore, we conclude that the questionnaire to be
distributed to voters with the general election ballot pamphlet
may not include a reference to the "Secretary of State" in the
mailing instructions.

With respect to whether the general election pamphlet
complies with the mass mailing provisions of the Act, if you wish
to submit a copy of the proposed pamphlet, we will be glad to
review it.

I trust this provides you with the information that you need.
If you have any further questions, you may contact us at (916)
322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin,
Acting General Counsel

[signature]

By: Margaret W. Ellison
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:MWE:aa
Enclosure

sections.analysis:
Section 89001 of the Act provides that "no newsletter or other mass mailing shall be sent at public expense." A mass mailing is defined as two hundred or more substantially similar pieces of mail. (Section 82041.5.) Regulation 18901 clarifies that the purpose of Section 89001 is to prevent elected officers from using public funds to send out newsletters and other mass mailings at public expense. A mass mailing may not be sent at public expense if it contains the name, office, photograph or any other reference to an elected officer affiliated with the agency producing or sending the mailing and is prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination or concert with the elected officer. (Regulation 18901(a).)

The use of the term "Secretary of State" in the mailing instructions is a reference to the elected officer. Regulation 18901 sets forth a series of limited exceptions. These exemptions include an exemption to permit reference to an elected officer in the letterhead or envelopes of the stationery of the agency. (Regulation 18901(b)(1), copy enclosed.) The exemption does not extend to permitting reference to the elected officer in mailing instructions.

Therefore, we conclude that the questionnaire to be distributed to voters with the general election ballot pamphlet may not include a reference to the "Secretary of State" in the mailing instructions.

With respect to whether the general election pamphlet complies with the mass mailing provisions of the Act, if you wish to submit a copy of the proposed pamphlet, we will be glad to review it.

I trust this provides you with the information that you need. If you have any further questions, you may contact us at (916) 322-5901.

QUESTION: Is it permissible to include the words "Secretary of State" in directions to voters for the return of a questionnaire which will be included in the November 1990 General Election Ballot Pamphlet?

CONCLUSION: The questionnaire may not include a reference to the "Secretary of State" in the mailing instructions.
